articles.title: JEFF BEZOS IN TALKS TO RAISE $100 BILLION FOR AI MANUFACTURING FUND
articles.summary: Jeff Bezos is in early talks to raise $100 billion for a new fund that would buy up manufacturing companies and use AI technology to accelerate their path to automation. The fund will aim to buy companies in major industrial sectors, including chipmaking, defense, and aerospace. Bezos is meeting up with some of the world's largest asset managers to raise funding for the project. He has traveled to the Middle East and Singapore to discuss the fund with investors.

articles.title: OPENAI PLANS LAUNCH OF DESKTOP ‘SUPERAPP' TO REFOCUS, SIMPLIFY USER EXPERIENCE
articles.summary: OpenAI plans to unify its ChatGPT app, Codex, and browser into a desktop superapp to simplify the user experience. The superapp will feature agentic AI capabilities that can work autonomously on users' computers to carry out a variety of tasks. Focusing on the superapp will enable OpenAI's teams to work closer together as it focuses its efforts around improving one central product.

articles.title: PARDONED FOR FRAUD, A CEO MOUNTS HIS COMEBACK: ‘WE CAN TRUST YOU NOW'
articles.summary: Trevor Milton was convicted of defrauding Nikola investors in 2022. He faced a four-year prison term, but Trump signed an unconditional pardon. Milton, along with his wife, had donated at least $3.2 million to Trump's 2024 election and to political groups and people in Trump's orbit. He is now raising funds for a new jet he claims will transform flying.

articles.title: DOGFIGHTING IN SPACE WON'T LOOK LIKE THE MOVIES, BUT THIS COMPANY WANTS IN ON IT
articles.summary: A startup called True Anomaly has created a war-ready satellite platform designed for mass production. The Jackal spacecraft is designed for agility and maneuverability and could be used for orbital combat. About the size of a refrigerator, the Jackal is essentially a flying fuel tank with room for thrusters and sensors that can rapidly turn, approach, and surveil other objects in orbit. True Anomaly seems well-positioned to win a contract for the Space Force's upcoming RG-XX program.

articles.title: UNBLOCKED: CONTEXT THAT SAVES YOU TIME AND TOKENS
articles.summary: Stop babysitting your coding agents. Unblocked [10] gives them the organizational knowledge to generate mergeable code. It pulls context from across your engineering stack, resolves conflicts, and cuts the rework cycle by delivering only what agents need for the task at hand.

articles.title: INTRODUCING COMPOSER 2
articles.summary: Composer 2 is now available in Cursor. It is frontier-level in coding, delivering large improvements on all measured benchmarks. The standard model is priced at $0.50/M input and $2.50/M output tokens. A faster variant, which has the same intelligence, will be priced at $1.50/M input and $7.50/M output tokens. The faster variant will be the default option in Cursor.

articles.title: READ LESS, STEER MORE
articles.summary: Don't treat AI output as code to read. If something doesn't work, just ask AI to rewrite the code exactly to the specifications. AI should be treated like a fast typist that is carrying out your will. If you know how you want something to look, make the AI do it the way you want - don't accommodate the AI.

articles.title: CHINA IS MOBILIZING THOUSANDS OF ONE-PERSON AI STARTUPS
articles.summary: Chinese cities are offering free apartments to attract 'one-person companies'. The plan also involves converting idle data centers into incubators to help grow the AI industry. Solo founders also receive free office space, discounted computing power, and special loans to build AI applications. While Silicon Valley's AI boom is being driven by venture capital funds, China is leaning on government policy and funding to mobilize participation from both industries and individuals.

articles.title: THE DISPLACEMENT OF COGNITIVE LABOR AND WHAT COMES AFTER
articles.summary: AI will eventually exceed human capability across most cognitive domains. It is already able to make engineers significantly more productive. Intelligence is now a commodity on tap. This post looks at the impacts of labor that harnessing intelligence will have. It covers AI's impacts on the job market, governance and geopolitics, and human nature.

articles.summary: To enable the community to get hands-on with NVIDIA Nemotron on Google Cloud, join the NVIDIA Nemotron model reasoning challenge on Kaggle [16], powered by G4 VMs!

articles.title: WE NEED TO TALK ABOUT AGENTS
articles.summary: When everything becomes an 'agent,' it's nearly impossible for buyers to tell what any given product actually does, how it fits into existing operations, how they should interact with it, or why it matters to their bottom line.

articles.title: BE INTENTIONAL ABOUT HOW AI CHANGES YOUR CODEBASE
articles.summary: Using the right patterns will lead to scalable codebases, which leads to faster iteration cycles and better software.

articles.title: XIAOMI LAUNCHES NEXT-GEN SU7 WITH 902 KM RANGE AND LIDAR, STILL UNDERCUTS TESLA
articles.summary: Xiaomi's SU7 electric sedan, which starts at around $33,000, has up to 902 km of CLTC range, standard LiDAR across all trims, and an 897V charging architecture on the top model.
